title: Athrixia asteroides
taxonomy: Athrixia asteroides is a species of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae. It belongs to the genus Athrixia and is native to the Western Cape province of South Africa. The plant is classified as a dicotyledonous species, meaning it has two cotyledons or seed leaves in its embryo. Within the Asteraceae family, Athrixia asteroides is part of the subfamily Asteroideae. This classification helps to organize and understand the evolutionary relationships among plant species within the Asteraceae family.

The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive problems. intro: Athrixia asteroides, commonly known as the yellow daisy bush, is a plant species belonging to the family Asteraceae. The plant has been traditionally used in South African folk medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antifungal activities, which contribute to its potential health benefits in treating wounds, respiratory infections, and digestive issues. The bioactive constituents of Athrixia asteroides include sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Athrixia asteroides include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are used to treat various health conditions and promote overall well-being.
